title: Building an AI-Powered Content Pipeline from Scratch
excerpt: How I designed and built an automated content processing workflow using NLP, careful evaluation, and a lot of edge-case handling.
content: The system has three stages: ingestion, processing, and routing.
contentHtml:
The Problem

Every content team hits manual tagging bottlenecks at scale.

Architecture Overview

The pipeline automates summarization, tagging, and routing with human review for low-confidence cases.

Routing Example

The routing layer stays intentionally small so confidence thresholds are visible during review.

def route_item(item):
    if item["confidence"] < 0.82:
        return "human_review"
    return item["channel"]
seoTitle: Building an AI-Powered Content Pipeline from Scratch
seoDescription: How I designed and built an automated content processing workflow using NLP, careful evaluation, and a lot of edge-case handling.
shareMessage: A practical walkthrough of building an AI-assisted content pipeline that can survive real-world edge cases.
featuredImageAlt: Hands on a piano keyboard beside an IDE dashboard

title: Design Systems That Actually Work
excerpt: Lessons from building and maintaining design systems across different teams. Spoiler: the hard part isn't components.
content: Adoption is the hard part, not component APIs.
contentHtml:
Why Most Design Systems Fail

Adoption and workflow fit determine whether systems survive.

What Actually Works

Start with tokens, then make the right thing the easy thing.
seoDescription: Lessons from building and maintaining design systems across different teams. Spoiler: the hard part isn't components.
shareMessage: Good design systems succeed when tokens, workflow fit, and decision clarity all line up.

title: What Playing Guitar Taught Me About Writing Code
excerpt: The surprising parallels between learning an instrument and becoming a better programmer.
content: Practice means isolating hard parts and improving deliberately.
contentHtml:
Practice vs. Performance

Skill growth comes from focused repetition, not passive repetition.

The Ensemble Effect

Great software teams, like bands, require listening and timing.
seoTitle: What Playing Guitar Taught Me About Writing Code
seoDescription: The surprising parallels between learning an instrument and becoming a better programmer.
shareMessage: Practice, listening, and timing matter in software teams as much as they do in music.
